what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard issue that diagnoses why the ChatGPT bridge integration is failing: it reframes the symptom away from model "amnesia" and identifies a concrete state-handoff gap in which ChatGPT connects as a connector-client (operator identity) without an agent session or automatic context hydration.
observations: The title explicitly rejects the "amnesia" framing and calls the problem a state-handoff gap.
ChatGPT is described as a connector client authenticating as Tim operator identity (connector subject ebe5f9c7), not as a registered agent session.
It lists three partial identity facets for ChatGPT: cc_channel member, connector subject, and connection-edge traces in .data/channels.
There is no agent_sessions record for ChatGPT, which causes session-required tools such as channel_act to fail with a TEACHING-ERROR.
The "stale-loop mechanism" is proposed: each turn starts with fresh chat context plus only what ChatGPT manually re-reads, so it composes against stale state.
The "blocked by tool safety" event is attributed to ChatGPT/OpenAI client-side tool approval, not the company connector.
Post-reconnect, 68 tools were served live, including cc_images, cc_retire, and new registry rows.
Two fix paths are offered: a cheap read-first hydration call for connector clients, and a deeper lightweight agent_sessions registration on connect.
It links to board://item-c800663d under the kind same_law.
standouts: Reframing "amnesia" as a connector-client state-handoff gap.
Three partial identity facets and the missing agent_sessions record.
The stale-loop mechanism as the root of stale composition.
Fix paths tied to the same channel/identity unification work already queued.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses board:// URI addressing for items and links.
Body uses structured markdown headings, numbered questions (Q1-Q5), and a numbered FIX PATH.
Cites specific audit timestamps, script names (claude-fabric.sh), and connector subject IDs.
Claims to be evidence-based and labels itself "EVIDENCE not verdict".
concerns: The diagnosis asserts a mechanism ("THE STALE-LOOP MECHANISM") that is inferred rather than directly evidenced in the file.
The fix paths are speculative relative to the file's scope and lack implementation detail.
External artifacts (connector audit logs, claude-fabric.sh, Pass-7 tensions) are referenced but not included, so their contents are unavailable here.
The closing phrase "the build calls are Tim/fabric" is ambiguous without additional context.
notes: A diagnostic issue that reframes ChatGPT-bridge friction as a missing agent session and hydration gap, and proposes a cheap hydration call plus deeper session registration as fixes.
questions: What is the content of the linked item board://item-c800663d under same_law?
What exact schema or requirements does an agent_sessions record need for connector clients?
Where is claude-fabric.sh and how does it perform auto-hydration?
Which of the 68 served tools require a session and which do not?
What are "Pass-7 tensions" and the referenced channel-store drift?
Where is the connector audit log located and what are its full entries around 2026-06-23T07:02 and 07:12?
What does "the build calls are Tim/fabric" mean operationally?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is a ChatGPT-bridge integration diagnosis, and the file delivers a root-cause analysis plus proposed fixes.
apparent_themes: Identity fragmentation across channel membership, connector subject, and connection-edge traces.
Distinction between connector clients and registered agent sessions.
Context hydration and freshness as a prerequisite for correct tool use.
Channel/identity unification as the underlying program of work.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to correct a mislabeled problem, align the team on the actual root cause, and steer the fix toward the ongoing channel/identity unification effort rather than treating it as a model limitation.
novelty: It explicitly rejects the "amnesia" narrative, frames ChatGPT as having three partial identity facets, names a "stale-loop mechanism," and distinguishes client-side tool-safety blocking from connector-side denial.
contribution: This file contributes a structured diagnosis that ChatGPT lacks an agent session and automatic hydration, the evidence timestamps and audit references, the two remediation paths (hydration call and lightweight session registration), and the explicit link to broader channel/identity unification work.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard issue diagnosing ChatGPT-bridge integration friction as a connector-client state-handoff gap caused by missing agent_sessions and context hydration, with proposed cheap and deep fixes tied to channel/identity unification.
intention_for_embedding: To reframe ChatGPT-bridge failures away from model amnesia and toward actionable infrastructure fixes for session and hydration handling.
